source_pr.title: test: fix unstable tests using failpoint
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: wjhuang2016 <huangwenjun1997@gmail.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

`go test -v -vet=off -p 20 -timeout 10m github.com/pingcap/tidb/executor`
There are so many fail tests!!!
The tests use failpoint, but these tests are not serial. 

### What is changed and how it works?



### Related changes



### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->


- No code

Side effects


### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

symptoms: Failpoint-enabled executor tests were unstable when suites ran non-serially because failpoints are global and can interfere across tests.
failure_signature: Cross-test interference from global failpoints when executor suites run concurrently (non-serial)
root_cause_explanation: Failpoints are global toggles that affect shared code paths. Several executor tests enabled failpoints but were registered under non-serial suites, so concurrent execution could observe each other's failpoint state and produce flaky failures. This PR stabilizes the suite by moving failpoint-dependent tests into `SerialSuites` (so they don't run concurrently with other tests) and by tightening error assertions to avoid nil/ordering races.
fix_pattern: Run failpoint-enabled tests in serial suites (and ensure failpoints are enabled/disabled within test scope) to prevent global failpoint state from leaking across concurrent tests.

evidence: evidence: PR title: fix unstable tests using failpoint.
evidence: adjusts failpoint-enabled tests to avoid cross-test interference (executor/executor_test.go, executor/slow_query_test.go).
